content:
Hello all, first time posting outside of newbie corner :)

I recently completed an almost 1:1 driver for BCM2835 HWRNG (for which I asked a lot of questions in newbie corner, thanks everyone who helped). In order to make it work I had to create a tiny abstraction around reset.h providing support for the basic reset_control struct and 3 functions. I wonder if it's good enough in order to submit a PR for it, so I'd like to hear your word on it so as to avoid spamming the GitHub repo.

reset.rs https://github.com/nikosmar/bcm2835_rust_driver/blob/main/modified_linux_files/rust/kernel/reset.rs
reset_control_get_optional_exclusive() in device.rs https://github.com/nikosmar/bcm2835_rust_driver/blob/main/modified_linux_files/rust/kernel/device.rs#L82
Please feel free to send the PR -- while many PRs will eventually have to go as patches through their lists, it is useful to know what others are working on.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Please feel free to send the PR -- while many PRs will eventually have to go as patches through their lists, it is useful to know what others are working on.

Alright I'll submit it soon!
